title: senecio ameghinoi
intro: Senecio ameghinoi, also known as Andean wormwood, is a plant species native to the Andes mountains in South America, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids. The health benefits of Senecio ameghinoi include its ability to protect the liver from damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of fever and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea, or made into a tincture, with caution due to its potential toxicity and the need for proper dosing.
